heading: Unique passport identifiers for covered sex offenders
label: 212b Unique passport identifiers for covered sex offenders

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Immediately after receiving a written determination from the Angel Watch Center that an individual is a covered sex offender, through the process developed for that purpose under , the Secretary of State shall take appropriate action under subsection (b).section 21507 of title 34

children.heading: Authority to use unique passport identifiers
children.label: b Authority to use unique passport identifiers

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided under paragraph (2), the Secretary of State shall not issue a passport to a covered sex offender unless the passport contains a unique identifier, and may revoke a passport previously issued without such an identifier of a covered sex offender.

children.heading: Authority to reissue
children.label: 2 Authority to reissue
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), the Secretary of State may reissue a passport that does not include a unique identifier if an individual described in subsection (a) reapplies for a passport and the Angel Watch Center provides a written determination, through the process developed for that purpose under , to the Secretary of State that the individual is no longer required to register as a covered sex offender.section 21507 of title 34

children.heading: Clarification with respect to continuing registration
children.label: 3 Clarification with respect to continuing registration
children.text: An individual may not be issued or reissued a passport without a unique identifier solely because the individual has moved or otherwise resides outside the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: is a sex offender, as defined in ; andsection 21503(f) of title 34

children.label: B
children.text: is currently required to register under the sex offender registration program of any jurisdiction;
the term “unique identifier” means any visual designation affixed to a conspicuous location on the passport indicating that the individual is a covered sex offender; and
the term “passport” means a passport book or passport card.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: d Prohibition
children.text: The Secretary of State, the Secretary of Homeland Security, and the Attorney General, and their agencies, officers, employees, and agents, shall not be liable to any person for any action taken under this section.

children.heading: Disclosure
children.label: e Disclosure
children.text: In furtherance of this section, the Secretary of State may require a passport applicant to disclose that they are a registered sex offender.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: f Effective date
children.text: This section shall take effect upon certification by the Secretary of State, the Secretary of Homeland Security, and the Attorney General, that the process developed and reported to the appropriate congressional committees under has been successfully implemented.section 21507 of title 34
